name: Equal Employment Opportunity Commission v. Abercrombie & Fitch Stores, Inc.
question: Can an employer be held liable under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 for refusing to hire an applicant based on a religious observance or practice if the employer did not have direct knowledge that a religious accommodation was required?
description: A case in which the Court held that an employer may be held liable under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act if an applicant can show that her need for an accommodation was a motivating factor in the employer’s decision not to hire her.
